q: Bo Bo overslept as he did not set his alarm clock. (Use the third conditional.)
a: If Bo Bo had set his alarm clock, he would not have overslept.

q: If she (study) harder, she (pass) the exam. (Use 'the third conditional'.) 2024
a: If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam.

q: She did not study regularly, and as a result, she did not pass the examination. (Use the third conditional.) 2024
a: If she had studied regularly, she would have passed the examination.

q: I did not know it was your birthday, so I did not buy you a present. (Use the third conditional.) 2024
a: If I had known it was your birthday, I would have bought you a present.

q: She didn't switch on the lights, so she fell over the chair. (Use the third conditional.) 2026
a: If she had switched on the lights, she would not have fallen over the chair.

q: I did not have enough money. I did not buy a car (Use 'the third conditional'.) 2025
a: If I had had enough money, I would have bought a car.

q: We did not save more money, and thus, we could not buy a bigger house. (Use 'the third conditional'.) 2026
a: We could have bought a bigger house if we had saved more money. (If ....... , .......)

q: Thu Thu didn't go to the get-together party because she had to work late. (Use the zero / first / second / third conditional.) 2025
a: If Thu Thu hadn't had to work late, she would have gone to the get-together party.

q: If you (follow) your teacher's guidelines, you (pass) the exam. (Use 'the third conditional'.) 2025
a: If you had followed your teacher's guidelines, you would have passed the exam.

q: She didn't check the map, so she got lost. (Use the third conditional.)
a: If she had checked the map, she wouldn't have gotten lost.

q: The wildfire started because someone at the campsite was careless. (Use the zero / first / second / third conditional.)
a: If someone at the campsite had not been careless, the wildfire would not have started.

q: The prices on the menu in that restaurant were very high. Therefore, we didn't go there. (Use the third conditional.)
a: If the prices on the menu had not been very high, we would have gone there.

q: If he (make) a stupid mistake, he (win) the competition. (Use 'the third conditional')
a: If he had made a stupid mistake, he would not have won the competition.

q: It rained heavily last night. We could not camp under the stars. (Use 'the third conditional'.)
a: If it had not rained heavily last night, we could have camped under the stars.

q: He did not practise enough, and as a result, he did not improve his skills. (Use the third conditional.)
a: If he had practised enough, he would have improved his skills.

q: He didn't call me because he didn't have my number. (Use the third conditional.)
a: If he had had my number, he would have called me.

q: I was ill and I did not go to my friend's birthday party. (Use the third conditional.) 2024
a: If I hadn't been ill, I would have gone to my friend's birthday party.

q: You didn't ask for directions. You got lost on the way. (Use 'zero/first/second/ third conditional'.) 2026
a: If you had asked for directions, you wouldn't have got lost on the way.

q: He went to that coffee shop, so he met his old friend. (Use 'the third conditional'.) 2025
a: If he hadn't gone to that coffee shop, he wouldn't have met his old friend.
